steps.title: Use symmetry of the roots
steps.content: The zeros are $x=-20$ and $x=-4$. For a quadratic, the vertex lies on the vertical line halfway between the two zeros.

steps.title: Average the zero locations
steps.content:
Find the midpoint of the two $x$-values.
$$\frac{-20+(-4)}{2}=\frac{-24}{2}=-12$$

steps.title: Read the requested coordinate
steps.content: The vertex is written as $(r,-64)$, so $r$ is the $x$-coordinate of the vertex. The answer is -12.
